title: Eriocoma atriplicifolia
intro: Eriocoma atriplicifolia, also known as Australian saltbush, is a shrubby plant native to Australia, characterized by its compact growth habit and succulent leaves. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of digestive issues and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Eriocoma atriplicifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are traditionally used to promote wound healing and alleviate symptoms of eczema and acne.
